title: Error parsing manifest for image
body: I tried to run the example from the documentation, unable to get data ![image](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/assets/79098669/d3e3345a-5253-416e-8b88-ec1a069892ff) Then I guessed it might be the architecture, but it still doesn't work When I specify the tag of the mirror, it may be fine, but some tags still can't get the data I tried to use https://github.com/containers/image in golang and it still doesn't solve my problem, why is that?

comments.body:
> I tried to use https://github.com/containers/image in golang and it still doesn't solve my problem, why is that?

Without specifics it’s impossible to say

---

With the `skopeo inspect` commands, on Mac you probably need `--override-os linux`, in addition to _possibly_ `--override-arch`, depending on your use case. Fedora doesn’t exist as macOS-native containers.

> With the `skopeo inspect` commands, on Mac you probably need `--override-os linux`, in addition to _possibly_ `--override-arch`, depending on your use case. Fedora doesn’t exist as macOS-native containers.

oh, my bad. It's working now. tks
